title: Graph Theory, Networks, Scheduling, and Routing · Quantity Clinic: Network Tradeoffs
summary: Design routes and schedules for deliveries and shared services. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int network tradeoffs increment in a network routing and scheduling analysis. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Compare efficiency, resilience, fairness, and capacity in network decisions.
Compute shortest paths and verify that edge assumptions fit the algorithm.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Network Tradeoffs evidence
steps.instruction: A model passes through (3, 11) and (7, 23). Calculate its average rate of change.
steps.why: A numerical result is not complete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3).

content.title: Before calculating
content.text: Predict the sign and approximate magnitude. Enter the requested quantity with a unit, then compare it with that prediction.

hints: Use Δoutput/Δinput = (23 − 11) / (7 − 3).
Keep full working precision and round only the final reported value.
The accepted result is within 0.001 of the exact value and includes units per input or per input.

steps.title: Defend the Network Tradeoffs model
steps.instruction: Write a concise evidence note that explains when Network Tradeoffs applies, what can fail, and how the changed case was verified.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.
